LM-Polygraph

LM-Polygraph
  • Welcome to LM-Polygraph’s documentation!
  • View page source

Welcome to LM-Polygraph’s documentation!

LM-Polygraph is a Python library that specialises on uncertainty estimation for LLMs.

Recent advancements in the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) have paved the way for a myriad of groundbreaking applications in various fields. However, a significant challenge arises as these models often ‘’hallucinate’’, i.e., fabricate facts without providing users an apparent means to discern the veracity of their statements.

Uncertainty estimation (UE) methods could be used to detect unreliable generations unlocking the safer, more responsible, and more effective use of LLMs in practice. However, as of now, UE methods for language models are a subject of bleeding-edge research with no easy-to-use implementations. In this work, we tackle this issue by introducing LM-Polygraph.

This program framework provides a battery of state-of-the-art UE methods for LLMs in text generation tasks with unified program interfaces in Python.

Check out the Basic usage section for further information, including several subsections: - How to conduct Installation the project. - Several jupyter notebooks for Quick start. - To reproduce expiremental results we provide innstructions for benchmarks in Benchmarks section.

Web demo illustration colud be found in Demo web application section. It is presented in subsection demo_illustration. Detailed information about modules is available in the Modules section.
